DuckSheet — Test Formulas for testdata.xlsx
===========================================
Recommended layout: add a new sheet named "DuckDB" to testdata.xlsx.
Put formulas below in column B (one per row, in order). Add a "Results" sheet for query output.
All paths below assume testdata.xlsx is open from its folder at:
  C:\Users\Administrator\source\repos\ducksheet\testdata\

Adjust the .duckdb path to wherever you want the database to live.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 1 — DUCK.CONNECT
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Register the database. Must be the first formula, above everything else.

  =DUCK.CONNECT("C:\Users\Administrator\source\repos\ducksheet\testdata\test.duckdb", "db1")

Expected: Registered: db1 → test.duckdb  [timestamp]

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 2 — DUCK.SEND (all three sheets)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Push each sheet into DuckDB. These recreate the tables on every recalc.

  =DUCK.SEND(sales!A1:N501,     "sales",    "db1")
  =DUCK.SEND(products!A1:M31,   "products", "db1")
  =DUCK.SEND(large!A1:O5001,    "large",    "db1")

Expected for sales:
  Loaded: sales — 500 rows, 14 cols
    order_id: VARCHAR
    order_date: TIMESTAMP
    close_date: TIMESTAMP
    rep_name: VARCHAR
    region: VARCHAR
    category: VARCHAR
    product_name: VARCHAR
    quantity: BIGINT
    unit_price: DOUBLE
    discount_pct: DOUBLE
    revenue: DOUBLE
    is_renewal: BOOLEAN
    status: VARCHAR
    notes: VARCHAR  [timestamp]

Expected for products:
  Loaded: products — 30 rows, 13 cols
    product_id: BIGINT
    sku: VARCHAR
    product_name: VARCHAR
    category: VARCHAR
    supplier: VARCHAR
    cost_price: DOUBLE
    list_price: DOUBLE
    stock_qty: BIGINT
    reorder_level: BIGINT
    is_active: BOOLEAN
    last_restocked: TIMESTAMP
    weight_kg: DOUBLE
    notes: VARCHAR  [timestamp]

Expected for large:
  Loaded: large — 5000 rows, 15 cols
    employee_id: BIGINT
    first_name: VARCHAR
    last_name: VARCHAR
    email: VARCHAR
    department: VARCHAR
    job_title: VARCHAR
    hire_date: TIMESTAMP
    salary: DOUBLE
    bonus_pct: DOUBLE
    is_fulltime: BOOLEAN
    city: VARCHAR
    years_exp: BIGINT
    performance_score: DOUBLE
    last_review_date: TIMESTAMP
    manager_id: BIGINT  [timestamp]

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 3 — DUCK.EXECUTE (DDL / views)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Build views from the loaded tables using the existing .sql file:

  =DUCK.EXECUTE("C:\Users\Administrator\source\repos\ducksheet\testdata\build_views.sql", "db1")

Expected: Created or replaced view: sales_summary  [timestamp]
  (two views created — sales_summary and rep_leaderboard)

Inline DDL — create a staging table and delete from it:

  =DUCK.EXECUTE("CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE staging AS SELECT * FROM sales WHERE status = 'Pending'", "db1")

Expected: Created or replaced table: staging  [timestamp]

  =DUCK.EXECUTE("DELETE FROM staging WHERE discount_pct IS NULL", "db1")

Expected: Deleted: N rows  [timestamp]

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 4 — DUCK.QUERY (SELECT results → Results sheet)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

All results write to the Results sheet. Space them out vertically so they don't overlap.

-- Basic SELECT (sanity check all columns came through)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT * FROM sales LIMIT 10", "Results!$A$1", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: 10 rows → Results!$A$1  [timestamp]

-- File-based query (uses summary_query.sql, which reads from the views built above)
  =DUCK.QUERY("C:\Users\Administrator\source\repos\ducksheet\testdata\summary_query.sql", "Results!$A$15", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: N rows → Results!$A$15  [timestamp]

-- Rep leaderboard (tests the second view)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT * FROM rep_leaderboard", "Results!$A$30", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: 8 rows → Results!$A$30  [timestamp]

-- Aggregation with date truncation (tests TIMESTAMP columns)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT CAST(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) AS INTEGER) AS yr, region, ROUND(SUM(revenue),2) AS total_revenue FROM sales GROUP BY yr, region ORDER BY yr, total_revenue DESC", "Results!$A$45", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: N rows → Results!$A$45  [timestamp]

-- JOIN between sales and products (tests cross-table SQL)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT s.category, s.product_name, p.cost_price, p.list_price, ROUND(AVG(s.unit_price),2) AS avg_sold_price FROM sales s JOIN products p ON s.product_name = p.product_name GROUP BY s.category, s.product_name, p.cost_price, p.list_price ORDER BY s.category, s.product_name", "Results!$A$65", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: N rows → Results!$A$65  [timestamp]

-- Boolean filter (tests BOOLEAN column)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt, ROUND(SUM(revenue),2) AS revenue FROM sales WHERE is_renewal = true GROUP BY status ORDER BY revenue DESC", "Results!$A$80", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: 4 rows → Results!$A$80  [timestamp]

-- NULL check (tests null handling in discount_pct)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, COUNT(discount_pct) AS has_discount, COUNT(*) - COUNT(discount_pct) AS no_discount FROM sales", "Results!$A$90", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: 1 rows → Results!$A$90  [timestamp]

-- Large table aggregation (5000 rows, tests scale)
  =DUCK.QUERY("SELECT department, job_title, COUNT(*) AS headcount, ROUND(AVG(salary),2) AS avg_salary, ROUND(AVG(performance_score),2) AS avg_score FROM large GROUP BY department, job_title ORDER BY department, avg_salary DESC", "Results!$A$95", "db1")

Expected: Query OK: N rows → Results!$A$95  [timestamp]

-- DESCRIBE (tests schema introspection via DUCK.QUERY)
  =DUCK.QUERY("DESCRIBE sales",    "Results!$H$1",  "db1")
  =DUCK.QUERY("DESCRIBE products", "Results!$H$20", "db1")
  =DUCK.QUERY("SHOW ALL TABLES",   "Results!$H$40", "db1")

Expected: schema grids at those addresses  [timestamp]

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 5 — DUCK.INSTRUCTIONS
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

No parameters. Widen the cell or expand the formula bar to read the output.

  =DUCK.INSTRUCTIONS()

Expected: full usage reference string

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
REFRESH REMINDERS
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Single formula:  click cell → F2 → Enter
Full refresh:    Ctrl+Alt+F9  (runs all formulas top to bottom)
